title: Aira notarisiana
intro: Aira notarisiana, also known as Notaris's hair grass, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Poaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for treating various health issues such as wounds, infections, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Aira notarisiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, Aira notarisiana is used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat ailments like fever, cough, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Aira notarisiana is a species within the family Poaceae and the genus Aira. It is a type of grass native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa.
